reasoning: The informal statement asks for all pairs (a,b) such that the line y = ax+b intersects y = ln(1+x²) in exactly one point. Since both are functions of x, an intersection point is uniquely determined by its x-coordinate, so "exactly one intersection point" is equivalent to "there is a unique x with ax+b = ln(1+x²)". The Lean encoding `{(a, b) | ∃! x : ℝ, a * x + b = Real.log (1 + x^2)}` uses `∃!` (unique existence) which is the standard Mathlib encoding for "exactly one". The equation correctly identifies the x-coordinates of intersection points. The statement asserts this set equals the provided solution set, which is exactly the "determine all" formulation. The formalization is strictly faithful.
